skill.name: finishing-a-development-branch
skill.description: Guides agents through the final steps after development work is complete, including tests, merge choices, pull requests, and cleanup.
skill.summary: Finish a development branch with test checks, merge or PR options, and controlled cleanup.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell command examples and a weak cryptography pattern. Review found no malware, prompt injection, or weak cryptography; the crypto hit at line 3 is a false positive. Risk remains medium because the skill instructs agents to run Git commands that can merge, push, delete branches, and remove worktrees.

medium_findings.title: Destructive Git Cleanup Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to delete a feature branch and remove a worktree after a typed confirmation. This is legitimate Git cleanup, but it can permanently remove local work if the branch or path is wrong.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The destructive commands are directly shown in the workflow. The skill also requires explicit confirmation, so malicious intent is not confirmed.

medium_findings.title: Remote Repository Publishing Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to push a branch and create a pull request. This can publish local code and PR metadata to a remote repository, which is expected but should remain user approved.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and affect remote state. They are normal development commands, with no evidence of credential theft or covert exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Local Merge Workflow Alters Base Branch
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to check out a base branch, pull updates, merge a feature branch, run tests, and delete the feature branch. These are valid release steps, but they can change repository history and local branch state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands clearly modify a repository. The surrounding instructions require test verification and a user choice, reducing abuse likelihood.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptography at the front matter description. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function, or crypto operation appears at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is descriptive metadata about development branches. The reviewed text contains no weak crypto usage.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence Backtick Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: Static analysis treated Markdown code fences as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The affected lines are documentation examples, not executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are fenced Markdown blocks or plain text templates. No Ruby code or hidden backtick execution was found.

low_findings.title: Benign Shell Substitution and Redirection Examples
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged stderr redirection, heredoc command substitution, and branch-name substitution. These appear inside documented Git examples and do not hide arbitrary command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell syntax is visible and tied to normal Git or GitHub CLI examples. No suspicious payload or obfuscation was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Force Delete Branch Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow includes git branch -D for confirmed discard actions. This command can remove an unmerged local branch.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicitly present. The risk is operational rather than malicious because confirmation is required.

dangerous_patterns.title: Worktree Removal Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow includes git worktree remove for cleanup. This can delete a checked-out worktree path if applied incorrectly.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cleanup command is explicit and affects the filesystem. The skill scopes it to selected completion paths.

dangerous_patterns.title: Git Push and Pull Request Creation
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow includes git push and gh pr create. These commands can publish branch contents and PR text to the remote host.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clear remote publishing actions. There is no evidence of a hidden endpoint or covert transfer.

content.value_statement: Development branches often stall after implementation because the final integration path is unclear. This skill guides test verification, merge or PR decisions, and branch cleanup with explicit user choices.
actual_capabilities: Requires test verification before merge, pull request, or cleanup actions.
Identifies a likely base branch using common Git branch names.
Presents four completion choices for merge, PR, keep, or discard.
Guides local merge flow with post-merge test verification.
Guides branch push and pull request creation with a simple PR body.
Requires typed confirmation before discard and destructive cleanup.
limitations: It does not run commands by itself; the host agent performs each step.
It assumes a Git repository and a compatible test command are available.
It does not resolve merge conflicts or failed tests automatically.
It relies on user confirmation for destructive branch and worktree actions.

use_cases.title: Complete a feature branch
use_cases.description: Run final checks and choose whether to merge, open a PR, keep the branch, or discard it.

use_cases.title: Prepare a pull request
use_cases.description: Push a completed branch and create a concise PR with summary and test plan sections.

use_cases.title: Clean up worktrees
use_cases.description: Remove temporary worktrees and branches only after the selected completion path allows cleanup.

prompt_templates.title: Finish this branch
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request after implementation is done.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the finishing-a-development-branch skill to verify tests and show me the completion choices for this branch.

prompt_templates.title: Create a pull request
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants remote review instead of a local merge.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the finishing-a-development-branch skill. If tests pass, help me push this branch and draft a pull request.

prompt_templates.title: Merge locally and clean up
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to integrate work into the base branch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the finishing-a-development-branch skill to merge this branch into the correct base branch, rerun tests, and clean up when safe.

prompt_templates.title: Audit branch completion options
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user wants a careful final decision.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the finishing-a-development-branch skill to inspect test status, detect the base branch, explain the four choices, and wait for my exact selection.

output_examples.input: Implementation is complete. Help me finish this branch.
output: Tests passed for the project test command.
Implementation complete. What would you like to do?
Options shown: merge locally, push and create PR, keep branch, or discard work.

output_examples.input: Discard this branch after checking it.
output: The skill lists the branch, commits, and worktree path before deletion.
It asks for the exact word discard before branch deletion or worktree cleanup.

output_examples.input: Create a PR for this completed work.
output: The branch is pushed to the remote repository.
A pull request is created with a short summary and test plan.

best_practices: Run the repository test suite before selecting any completion path.
Confirm the base branch before merging or deleting the feature branch.
Use typed confirmation for discard actions and verify the worktree path.
anti_patterns: Do not merge, push, or discard when tests are failing.
Do not delete a branch or worktree without explicit user confirmation.
Do not clean up a worktree when the selected option keeps the branch as-is.

faq.question: Does this skill run Git commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It gives the host agent a workflow. The agent should still ask before destructive or publishing actions.

faq.question: What happens if tests fail?
faq.answer: The workflow stops and reports the failures. It does not proceed to merge, PR creation, or cleanup.

faq.question: Can it create a pull request?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a GitHub CLI flow for pushing a branch and creating a pull request.

faq.question: Can it delete local work?
faq.answer: Yes. The discard and cleanup paths can delete branches or worktrees, so they require explicit confirmation.

faq.question: Does it support Git worktrees?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks for worktree use and removes the worktree only for selected cleanup paths.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for shared repositories?
faq.answer: It can be used safely with review. Users should confirm branch names, remotes, and PR text before publishing.
